title: iris reticulata subsp. isikgunerae
intro: Iris reticulata subsp. isikgunerae, also known as Isikgunerae iris, is a subspecies of the iris reticulata, a flowering plant native to Turkey. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and providing antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's rhizomes and bulbs are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
